title: Kubernetes with Minikube (Series 2: 10/10): Debugging Like an SRE
summary: Create real failure scenarios and debug them systematically — the final part of our intermediate series.

title: Kubernetes with Minikube (Series 2: 9/10): RBAC and Security
summary: Learn RBAC, Service Accounts, and security contexts — controlling who can do what in your cluster.

title: Kubernetes with Minikube (Series 2: 8/10): Helm Chart Packaging
summary: Learn to package, template, and share Kubernetes configurations using Helm.

title: Kubernetes with Minikube (Series 2: 7/10): StatefulSets and Databases
summary: Deploy stateful workloads using StatefulSets, with Redis as our hands-on example.

title: Kubernetes with Minikube (Series 2: 6/10): Jobs and CronJobs
summary: Explore Jobs, CronJobs, and Init Containers — workloads that run to completion rather than forever.

title: Kubernetes with Minikube (Series 2: 5/10): Networking Internals
summary: Look inside Kubernetes networking: kube-proxy, iptables, CoreDNS, and Network Policies.

title: Kubernetes with Minikube (Series 2: 4/10): Persistent Storage
summary: Give your applications storage that survives pod restarts using Volumes, PersistentVolumes, and PVCs.

title: Kubernetes with Minikube (Series 2: 3/10): Secrets and Configuration
summary: Explore Secrets, ConfigMaps in depth, and the security realities you need to know.

title: Kubernetes with Minikube (Series 2: 2/10): Namespaces and Resource Quotas
summary: Organise a cluster for multiple teams and environments using Namespaces and Resource Quotas.

title: Kubernetes with Minikube (Series 2: 1/10): Rolling Updates and Rollbacks
summary: Explore how Kubernetes replaces your application without downtime and how to undo mistakes.

title: Kubernetes with Minikube (1/10: 10/10): Production Readiness
summary: Add health checks (probes), resource limits, and observability to your services — the features that separate a demo from a production-ready system.

title: Kubernetes with Minikube (1/10: 9/10): Ingress and HTTP Routing
summary: Set up an Ingress controller for path-based and host-based HTTP routing, replacing random NodePort numbers with clean URLs.

title: Kubernetes with Minikube (1/10: 8/10): Service-to-Service Communication
summary: Configure services to talk to each other using Kubernetes DNS, ConfigMaps, and NGINX reverse proxying.

title: Kubernetes with Minikube (1/10: 7/10): Multi-Service Architecture
summary: Deploy a second application alongside the first and learn how Kubernetes isolates services using labels, selectors, and independent scaling.

title: Kubernetes with Minikube (1/10: 6/10): Scaling and Self-Healing
summary: Scale your app to multiple replicas, watch Kubernetes automatically replace crashed pods, and see load balancing in action.

title: Kubernetes with Minikube (1/10: 5/10): YAML and Declarative Configuration
summary: Transition from CLI commands to YAML manifests — the critical shift to infrastructure as code and the real way to work with Kubernetes.

title: Kubernetes with Minikube (1/10: 4/10): Exposing Your App
summary: Learn about Services, NodePort, and port-forwarding to make your application accessible from outside the cluster.

title: Kubernetes with Minikube (1/10: 3/10): Your First Deployment
summary: Deploy your first application and learn about Pods and Deployments — the core building blocks of everything you run on Kubernetes.

title: Kubernetes with Minikube (1/10: 2/10): Setting Up Your Local Cluster
summary: Install Minikube and kubectl on macOS, start your first Kubernetes cluster, and explore what's already running inside it.

title: Kubernetes with Minikube (1/10: 1/10): What is Kubernetes?
summary: Build a mental model of containers, orchestration, and Kubernetes architecture before touching any tools — the foundation for the entire series.

title: Hands-on: Setting up a Local Cluster with KinD
summary: A step-by-step guide to running Kubernetes locally using Docker container nodes. Perfect for testing CNI and Ingress controllers.
